name: Avoid Mental Mapping
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Single-letter or cryptic variable names require readers to constantly translate them into the real concept they represent, increasing cognitive load and making code harder to review and maintain.
steps: Replace single-letter variable names with names that express the concept they represent.
Reserve i, j, k only for small, obvious loop counters where no other names can conflict.
Prefer problem-domain or solution-domain terminology over arbitrary shorthand.
Verify that each name is self-explanatory without needing to mentally decode it.
Refactor names whenever you find yourself translating a variable to understand what it holds.
